script_text: Two-syllable words often have long vowel sounds.
Breaking the word into pieces helps us read them.
Base has a long A. The silent E makes it long.
Ball has a short sound. Both pieces together? Baseball!
Rain has the AI team. Long A sound!
Bow has OW. Another long sound!
Split. Read each piece. Then blend them back together!
Now you are ready to read two-syllable long vowel words!
